# GitHub Copilot Instructions for Emmet.net Project

Welcome to the Emmet.net project! This document provides guidelines and best practices for using GitHub Copilot effectively within this repository.

## Business Context

This project is a Microsoft Visual Studio extension that adds functionality from a popular front end development emmet plugin to the Visual Studio IDE. Embedded emmet core javascript library is executed using V8 engine and is exposed to Visual Studio editor as a set of commands and keyboard shortcuts. The extension is built using C# and .NET, with a focus on performance and seamless integration with the Visual Studio environment.

## General Guidelines

- **Code Quality:** Ensure all code generated by Copilot follows the project's coding standards and conventions.
- **Testing:** Write or update tests for any new features or bug fixes. Place tests `src/Emmet..Tests` project.
- **Documentation:** Add or update documentation for new code, especially public APIs and complex logic.
- **Security:** Avoid committing secrets, credentials, or sensitive data. Use configuration files and environment variables as appropriate.
- **Review:** Always review Copilot suggestions for correctness, security, and maintainability before committing.

## Project Structure

- **Extension:** Visual Studio extension code is under `src/Emmet` folder. It targets .NET Framework and cannot use modern C# features and NuGet packages.
- **Tests:** Unit tests are located in the `src/Emmet.Tests` project that uses modern .NET and `TUnit` library. It does not have direct reference to the extension project, so all required files are included as links to the extension folder.
- **Docs:** Project documentation is in the `docs/` folder and readme file.

## Coding Guidelines

### C#

- Prefer explicit variable types.
- Use target-typed new expressions.

## Unit Testing

- Use existing unit tests as an example for the new code.
- All tests for C# code should be located in `src/Emmet.Tests/` folder.
- Unit test method names should follow snake case convention, but first letter of the method name should be in upper case.
- Class with unit tests for C# class should have the same name with `Tests` postfix.
- Unit tests classes should use `TUnit` library.
- Assertions should be implemented using `Shouldly` library.
- For moqs and stubs generation use NSubstitue library.

## Copilot Usage Tips

- Use Copilot to generate boilerplate code, unit tests, and documentation comments.
- For C# code, follow the existing style in each project (see `stylecop.json` and `.editorconfig`).
- When in doubt, refer to the README files in each major folder for more details.

## Commit Messages

- Write clear, descriptive commit messages summarizing the change, prefix GitHub issue key if any.
- Reference related issues or pull requests when applicable.

## Pull Requests

- Ensure all checks pass before requesting a review.
- Provide a summary of changes and testing steps in the PR description.

## Additional Resources

- [Project README](../readme.md)

---

_This file is intended to help contributors use GitHub Copilot effectively and consistently in the Emmet.net project._
